reasoning: On March 1, 2025, Brazilian President Lula da Silva personally invited Yamandú Orsi to the BRICS summit in Rio de Janeiro during Orsi's inauguration ceremonies in Montevideo. Shortly thereafter, on March 3, 2025, the major Uruguayan national newspaper 'El País' reported that Orsi "will attend" (Concurrirá) the BRICS summit as an invited guest (search_2_result_6). This report, appearing in the context of an official briefing on the new president's immediate diplomatic agenda, functions as public confirmation from sources close to the presidency. Subsequent reporting in April 2025 (search_2_result_1) continued to list Orsi as an attending participant. Given Orsi's ideological alignment with Lula, Uruguay's active pursuit of membership in the BRICS New Development Bank, and the summit's location in a neighboring country, the announcement of attendance is highly likely and was effectively confirmed in early March.
